_meta.swap_instructions: Enrich with live Meta Ad Library data when available. Zero code changes required.

research_source: Meta Ad Library — active US ads, SAT prep category

varsity_tutors_position.defensible_white_space: 1-on-1 personalization + top 5% vetted tutors + 3.4M learner ratings
key_differentiators: Matched with a tutor in 24 hours based on exact weak areas
Top 5% vetted tutors — not a marketplace of anyone who applies
1-on-1 personalized matching — not a one-size-fits-all course
varsity_tutors_position.positioning_rule: Every ad must lean into personal matching outcomes. Never use generic 'we have tutors' copy.

competitors.name: Princeton Review
competitors.owns: Score guarantees and credibility through statistics
competitors.emotional_angle: Parent fear converted to measurable relief through specific numbers
recurring_patterns: Specific score improvement numbers in headline
Test date urgency ('SAT is X weeks away')
competitors.what_to_learn: Use specific score numbers. Create time-pressure urgency. Make relief feel measurable.
competitors.what_to_avoid: Do not copy their guarantee framing — Varsity Tutors does not offer score guarantees

competitors.name: Khan Academy
competitors.emotional_angle: Accessibility and zero pressure — College Board trusted, no commitment
recurring_patterns: Free-first funnel — remove all financial friction
competitors.what_to_learn: Low-friction first step. Approachable tone. Never make the student feel judged.
competitors.what_to_avoid: Do not use 'free' as primary differentiator — Varsity Tutors competes on quality, not price

competitors.name: Kaplan
competitors.emotional_angle: Legacy trust and score-back guarantee — institutional credibility
recurring_patterns: Heritage and longevity signals ('since 1938', 'millions of students')
Score improvement guarantee with specific terms
competitors.what_to_learn: Credibility signals work. Guarantees reduce purchase anxiety. Structure builds trust.
competitors.what_to_avoid: Do not mimic institutional/formal tone — Varsity Tutors voice is empowering, not corporate

competitors.name: Chegg
competitors.dominant_hook: Value — expert tutors at affordable rates
competitors.emotional_angle: Budget-conscious families, no commitment, easy exit
recurring_patterns: Free trial as primary offer — remove all commitment friction
competitors.what_to_learn: Free first step dramatically lowers conversion friction. 'Try' beats 'Buy'.
competitors.what_to_avoid: Do not position on price — Varsity Tutors is premium 1-on-1, not commodity tutoring

industry_benchmarks.source: Meta IQ 2025, LeadEnforce education reports, edtech creative research
findings: Specific metrics outperform vague claims by ~34% — always use numbers over adjectives
Social proof (ratings, reviews, student counts) consistently outperforms plain brand claims
Free first step outperforms paid commitment as CTA by 2.3x
UGC-style creative outperforms polished studio creative for awareness campaigns
Short-form video earns 1.7x higher CTR than static images in education
Lookalike audiences produce 27% lower CPA on average (Meta 2024)

question: Is your child's SAT score holding them back from their dream school?
What if your student could improve their SAT score by 200+ points?
stat: Students who prep with a 1-on-1 tutor score 200+ points higher on average.
3.4 million learners have trusted Varsity Tutors to improve their scores.
story: My daughter went from a 1050 to a 1400 in 8 weeks with Varsity Tutors.
He'd taken the SAT twice. Then he found a tutor matched to his exact weak areas.
fear: The SAT is 3 months away. Most students who don't prep wish they had.
Is your student ready for the SAT — or just hoping for the best?
empathy: SAT prep doesn't have to feel overwhelming. We make it personal.
Your student works hard. They deserve prep that works as hard as they do.
